channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: A universal all-dry microfabrication method for sensitive electronic materials via an inorganic molecular lithographic mediator
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68593-z

Conventional organic polymeric resists and chemical solutions used in microlithographic techniques can damage sensitive materials. Here, the authors report the application of inorganic selenium molecules as a lithographic mediator and encapsulation layer during the processing of various sensitive materials, such as perovskites and black phosphorus.
item.dc:title: A universal all-dry microfabrication method for sensitive electronic materials via an inorganic molecular lithographic mediator
item.dc:creator: Cheng Zeng
Yongshan Xu
Xinqi Wei
Jianing Liang
Zongdong Sun
Xiaoyu He
Gaofeng Du
Shuhao Wang
Yao Liu
Xing Zhou
Yuan Li
Kailang Liu
Ying Ma
Tianyou Zhai
Huiqiao Li

item.title: Polarization-modulated programmable photovoltaic performance of a designed ferroelectric heterojunction
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68853-y

The authors design a Pt/CuInP2S6/graphene heterostructure that achieves polarity-switchable photovoltaic current modulated by ferroelectric polarization. This enables programmable in-sensor computing, achieving high-performance visual tasks.
item.dc:title: Polarization-modulated programmable photovoltaic performance of a designed ferroelectric heterojunction
item.dc:creator: Meijiao Men
Zunyi Deng
Zijing Zhao
Lingchen Liu
Yuanyuan Cui
